data.title: Unit Corporation declares $1.25 dividend
data.description:
• Unit Corporation (OTCQX:UNTC) declared $1.25/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 17.78%
  • Payable March 28; for shareholders of record March 18; ex-div March 18.
  • See UNTC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Freddie Mac announces sale via auction of ~$290M worth of non-performing loans
data.description:
• Federal Home Loan Mortgage (OTCQB:FMCC) announced on Friday a sale via auction of ~$290M worth of non-performing loans.
  • The NPLs consist of seasoned, deeply delinquent residential first-lien whole loans held in the organization's mortgage-related investments portfolio.

data.title: Modine announces $100M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Modine (NYSE:MOD) on Friday announced a share repurchase program to repurchase up to $100 million of the company's outstanding common stock.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq plans to roll out 24-hour trading in H2 2026
data.description:
Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) is planning to introduce round-the-clock trading on its U.S. stock exchange by the back half of 2026, pending regulatory approval, Tal Cohen, the company's president, said on Friday.

NDAQ is seeking to meet growing global demand for U.S. retail stock trading, Cohen noted in a post on LinkedIn, while warning of risks tied to elevated volatility and potentially higher transaction costs during lightly traded overnight hours.

"While trading activities beyond the traditional hours have increased in recent years through off-exchange venues, such as Alternative Trading Systems (ATSs) and broker-dealer platforms, liquidity remains significantly lower during these hours," he explained.

The move mirrors efforts at other brokerages and exchanges, such as Robinhood Markets (HOOD) and Interactive Brokers (IBKR). Last month, Charles Schwab (SCHW) was said to have expanded its overnight trading to all of its retail clients.

NDAQ shares ticked up 0.1% in premarket trading.

data.title: February nonfarm payrolls stay solid, even as federal government employment starts to drop
data.description:
Nonfarm payrolls climbed 151K in January, compared with +125K in January, the Bureau of Labor Statistics said on Friday. With the consensus at 160K, the headline number is roughly in line with expectations. Overall, it indicates a still solid labor market.

Notably, the number of jobs added in January was revised down from +143K in its initial estimate.

The unemployment rate edged up to 4.1% from 4.0% and topped the 4.0% consensus. 

Already, the data is reflecting DOGE-driven government workforce cuts, with federal government employment down 10K, which was widely anticipated, said Chris Lau, Investing Group Leader for DIY Value Investing. While federal government employment dropped in February, local governments added 20K jobs and state government employment rose by 1K, the data showed.

Employment trended up in health care (+52K), financial activities (+21K), transportation and warehousing (+18K), and social assistance (+11K), the BLS said.

"For investors, the job hiring [in financial activities] contradicts the recent stock declines," Lau said. "Bank of America (BAC) topped at $48 to close below its 200-day moving average. JPMorgan (JPM) and Wells Fargo (WFC) are also lower. Markets are anticipating an economic slowdown amid tariffs."

Average hourly earnings increased 0.3% in February, matching the consensus and cooling from the 0.5% increase logged in January. During the month, the average workweek for all employees on private nonfarm payrolls was unchanged at 34.1 hours, the Bureau of Labor Statistics said.

"The US jobs report for February is basically in line with the consensus forecasts, with one qualification," said noted economist Mohamed A. El-Erian in a post on X. "The one qualification is the slip in labor force participation, from 62.6% to 62.4%. With this 'no news' report, the focus will shift quickly to what will happen next."

"Employment did not change much last month. However, industries like mining, oil, and gas may weaken as economic activity slows. Stocks like Freeport-McMoRan (FCX), Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF), and Exxon (XOM) are trading near their 52-week lows," Investing Group Leader Lau said.

"The solid expansion in payrolls in February highlights that the negative impact of tariff hikes and policy uncertainty on economic activity at the macro level is not going to be felt for quite a while," said Fitch Ratings Chief Economist Brian Coulton. "A number of survey indicators have deteriorated and the recent jump in import volumes ahead of tariff hikes could weigh on growth temporarily in the first quarter, but the more immediate impact of tariff hikes will be on prices and costs."

Bankrate Senior Economic Analyst Mark Hamrick is waiting to see how the economic picture unfolds. "The survey period for this report is around the 12th of the month. Much has happened since then, including the more than 60K federal job cut announcements as counted by the outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas. We’ll need to continue to monitor other data, including unemployment claims, in the coming weeks and months," he said.

data.title: BigBear.ai tumbles as Q4 results weigh on shares; Cantor Fitzgerald lowers price target
data.description:
• BigBear.ai (NYSE:BBAI) was in focus on Friday after the artificial intelligence company reported fourth-quarter results, prompting Cantor Fitzgerald to lower its price target on the stock.
  • Shares fell 15% in premarket trading.
  • "We are reiterating our Overweight rating on BigBear.ai (NYSE:BBAI) and lowering our 12-month price target to $6.00 from $8.00 following underwhelming 4Q24 top-line growth, lower-than-expected guidance, and Federal uncertainty," analyst Jonathan Ruykhaver wrote in a note to clients.
  • Ruykhaver said BigBear.ai saw positive deal momentum, including several "major" wins with the U.S. federal government, which helped its $480M backlog. In addition, the company announced a debt swap, changing a part of its existing 6% convertible senior notes due 2026 for new 6% convertible senior secured notes due 2029.
  • "We view this exchange as a prudent move to address near-term balance sheet concerns and provide greater financial flexibility over the coming years," Ruykhaver added.

data.title: Baidu prices $2B exchangeable bonds offering
data.description:
Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) has announced the pricing of its US$2B in aggregate principal amount of exchangeable bonds due 2032.

The bonds were offered in offshore transactions outside the U.S. to certain non-U.S. persons and will reference ordinary shares of Trip.com Group (NASDAQ:TCOM) that are listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

The bond will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount of the bonds will not accrete. It will mature on March 12, 2032, unless repurchased, redeemed, or exchanged in accordance with their terms prior to such date.

The initial exchange ratio will be 1,107.0457 Trip.com shares per US$100,000 principal amount of bonds (which is equivalent to an initial exchange price of ~HK$702.13 per Trip.com share).

The company expects to close the offering on or about March 12, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures rise as labor report comes in lower than expected
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday as nonfarm payrolls rose less than expected. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.93%.

The February nonfarm payrolls rose slightly less than expected and came in at +151K vs. +160K consensus.

U.S. stocks were slammed lower Thursday, with the S&P 500 marking its lowest close since September as pressure from tech stocks, labor market weakness, and tariff-policy uncertainty took their toll on large-cap equities.

“Fierce cold winds were again blowing in U.S. equity markets yesterday, with ongoing trade policy volatility sending the S&P 500 to its worst day of 2025 for a second time this week,” Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

"Having imposed aggressive taxes on U.S. consumers at the start of the week, US President Trump partially retreated from taxing Mexican and Canadian imports yesterday (assuming goods are compliant with the revised NAFTA). The taxes are delayed until 2 April, but markets will likely price in more retreats," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Broadcom (AVGO) shares rose 12.7% in premarket trade after the semiconductor and software giant reported first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations and suggested artificial intelligence spending has not slowed down.

"Today’s U.S. employment report will not really reflect government job losses because the government is not a significant employer and because timing will push the losses into later months. The main future employment hit is likely to be from private sector businesses that depend (directly or indirectly) on government employment," Donovan added. 

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also set to speak later in the day today.

data.title: SanDisk in spotlight as Cantor starts with Overweight rating
data.description:
• SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) was in the spotlight on Friday as Cantor Fitzgerald started coverage on the memory maker with an Overweight rating, following the split of Western Digital (WDC).
  • "We see numerous positive catalysts ahead, including a recovery in the NAND market in 2HCY25, elongated cycles driven by build-to-demand and supply-side actions limiting bit growth, a strong balance sheet with a net cash position expected in the coming year, a compelling through-cycle target financial model, strong FCF generation ability, and an industry-leading capital intensity advantage, cost structure, and roadmap," analyst C.J. Muse wrote in a note to clients.
  • In addition to an Overweight rating, Muse put a $60 price target on SanDisk. 
  • Despite the Overweight rating, Muse acknowledged there are some risks, including the potential for the recovery in the second-half of 2025 being "more muted" than expected; a more aggressive Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF); and the pending sale of Western Digital's remaining 20% stake in SanDisk.
  • "But all together, we find the risk/reward profile positively biased," Muse added.
